MCR_2.003.text: A judge is disqualified when the judge cannot impartially hear a case, including when there have been ex parte communications.
MCR_2.003.relevance: Rusco's ex parte contacts with HealthWest and Emily's side taint the entire proceeding — grounds for disqualification.

CANON_2_RULE_2.9.text: A judge shall not initiate, permit, or consider ex parte communications. Court staff acting on judge's behalf are subject to same restrictions.
CANON_2_RULE_2.9.relevance: Rusco, as FOC acting under court authority, is bound by Canon 2 restrictions on ex parte contacts.

MCL_552.507.text: FOC shall investigate and make recommendations regarding custody, parenting time, and support. FOC must serve copies of recommendations on all parties.
MCL_552.507.relevance: Rusco failed to include Andrew in communications and bypassed proper service requirements.

MCL_552.501.text: Establishes FOC duties including investigation of custody matters. FOC must act impartially.
MCL_552.501.relevance: Rusco's one-sided communications demonstrate bias in FOC role.

MCR_3.208.text: FOC has duty to investigate and report to the court. Parties must receive copies of all FOC communications with the court.
MCR_3.208.relevance: Emailing subpoena to judge rather than serving through clerk violates MCR service requirements.

While courts are supposed to be gender-neutral, some fathers feel that the system can lean in favor of the mother. Be prepared to counter this by focusing on facts and evidence rather than emotional appeals.

Order any additional relief the Court deems appropriate to ensure fairness in the child support arrangement and protect Lincoln’s well-being by allowing me more time and financial flexibility to focus on my son.

Litigation and the Judicial Process  1 LITIGATION AND THE JUDICIAL PROCESS Traditionally, the law school courses most relevant to someone contemplating a career as a litigator have been the courses focusing on the "law" applied by courts in
Ensure that you request a Friend of the Court (FOC) review of your case, particularly if Emily’s PPO or financial situation affected parenting time.
FOC Child Support Review: Request a FOC review of child support calculations to ensure that errors are identified and corrected, particularly in light of your proposed 50/50 arrangement.

foc_authority_entries.text: Change of circumstances must be established by proper evidence before modifying custody. FOC recommendations must be grounded in evidence.
foc_authority_entries.relevance: FOC accepted change-of-circumstances arguments without evidentiary support.
foc_authority_entries.filing: Lane A: Custody; Lane F: COA

foc_authority_entries.text: Circuit court judges have general superintending control over FOC. FOC is arm of the court, accountable to judiciary.
foc_authority_entries.relevance: McNeill's failure to supervise FOC = additional judicial misconduct ground.
foc_authority_entries.filing: Lane E: Judicial Misconduct; Lane G: MSC

foc_authority_entries.text: Friend of Court — Sets forth procedures for FOC recommendations, reports, and referee hearings. FOC must follow established procedures.
foc_authority_entries.relevance: FOC procedural requirements Barnes violated — failure to follow MCR 3.208 investigation standards.
foc_authority_entries.filing: Lane A: Custody; Lane F: COA 366810

foc_authority_entries.text: Hearing Procedures — Court shall hold evidentiary hearing on contested custody/parenting matters. FOC recommendations are not dispositive.
foc_authority_entries.relevance: McNeill relied on FOC recommendations without holding proper evidentiary hearings.
foc_authority_entries.filing: Lane A: Custody; Lane F: COA

foc_authority_entries.citation: US Const amend XIV; Const 1963, art 1, § 17
foc_authority_entries.text: Due process requires fair procedures. FOC recommendations that deny parenting time without adequate investigation violate procedural due process.
foc_authority_entries.relevance: 567+ days separation based partly on FOC failure to investigate = due process violation.
foc_authority_entries.filing: Lane A: Custody; Lane F: COA; Lane G: MSC; Federal §1983
